original_question:
Triangle $\triangle ABC$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $AB = 6, AC = 4,$ and $\angle BAC = 90^\circ.$ Let $D$ be the reflection across $\overline{BC}$ of the centroid of $\triangle ABC. {}$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $1, 2, 3,$ and $r$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $A, B, C,$ and $D,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $r$ can be written as $\tfrac mn,$ where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m+n.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

entries.original_incomplete_question: Triangle $\triangle DEF$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $DE = [BLANK], DF = [BLANK],$ and $\angle EDF = 90^\circ.$ Let $G$ be the reflection across $\overline{EF}$ of the centroid of $\triangle DEF.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $2, 3, 4,$ and $s$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $D, E, F,$ and $G,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $s$ can be written as $\tfrac{p}{q},$ where $p$ and $q$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $p+q.$

variants.variant: In plane \mathcal P, triangle \triangle DEF has sides $DE = [BLANK], DF = [BLANK],$ and the angle $\angle EDF = 90^\circ.$ Let $G$ denote the reflection of the centroid of triangle \triangle DEF across the line segment $\overline{EF}.$ There are four spheres with radii $2, 3, 4,$ and $s$, all positioned on the same side of \mathcal P, which are tangent to \mathcal P at points $D, E, F,$ and $G,$ respectively. These spheres also touch another plane \mathcal T and are located on the same side of \mathcal T. The value of $s$ can be expressed as $\tfrac{p}{q},$ where $p$ and $q$ are coprime positive integers. Calculate $p+q.$
